1.right:
The conquest of the fortress of Constantinople in 1453 AD

(Reign of Mehmed II, 1453 - 1481)
In Peshawar, the Ghazi King Muhammad I also built for the conquest of Constantinople, known as the Anatolian Fortress. When Muhammad II ascended the throne, he also took on a similar ambition. To fulfill this desire, on the European side of the city, he prepared this in three months. Thousands of laborers were put to work. In these three months, he prepared this. On this large tower, a large cannon was placed, and four hundred soldiers were also appointed there, and the order was given that every passing ship should be taxed.
The construction of this fortress began in April 1453. Constantinople is triangular in shape. It is surrounded by water on two sides and land on one side. For this reason, a moat was prepared on the land side, which was one hundred yards wide. The width of the land is six miles. King Constantine, for the purpose of military operations and for the defense of this place, brought Greek soldiers to the moat on his own initiative and threw them at the Turks. But it soon became clear to him that this kind of work was very damaging. After a while, they attacked the fortress walls and from there they would bombard the enemy with arrows and cannonballs. On the other hand, the Turks took shelter from the digging of trenches and slowly advanced. The Sultan's cannon, which was very famous at that time, and the fire of other cannons spread to the fortress walls. And with the slow firing of these cannons, the battlefield was made like the Day of Judgment, and the weak points were broken. In addition, young Turks would go under the gates and reach the edge of the moat.
1.left:
To fill the ditch, they cut down trees, piled up cannonballs in a heap, and the sacrificing youths created such a commotion that those who were able to see the enemy's strength would fall into the ditch, and in the blink of an eye, they would be buried under piles of dirt. It is said that at that time, death was more desirable than life, as they had already sold themselves for gold. This was the reason why the mother's silence was cast upon herself. The work of the besiegers was to dig the ditch, and the work of the besieged was to clear the ditch, which had become a daily battle. Besides this, the Sultan adopted other methods, but until the siege was fully completed, both dry and wet, it was difficult to capture the city. Attacking from the direction of the dam was impossible, as large ships were stationed on the ground on that side. There was a fear that the enemy might attack from the sea. At this time, Sultan Muhammad used his intellect and came up with a strange plan:
He transported his light ships and Turkish equipment across the Bosphorus waters to the dry land, to the large part of the port. The distance between the two seas is ten miles, because the entire area is deep and trees were cut down and stood in places. It was astonishing how, to transport ships on dry land, they built platforms, and on top of them, they placed logs and thick planks so that the ships could be moved. These ships and boats were placed between the Royal Palace and the Alala mountains. It is said that two sailors were on each boat and ship, and they steered themselves. In one night, the ships and boats entered the port waters like a flock of birds. To go to the front was to sacrifice oneself and become a martyr. The company's elders and such sons were ready, but the Sultan prepared a single boat in the water and a large cannon.

The meaning of Rai is neither Rai Bahadur nor Rai Sena. Who, in earnest, Sir John Simon
with splendor is the founder of the camp. Rather, the meaning and purpose of Rai is that magnificent
gift and grant of the generous Lord, upon which the human ascent and perfection, of reform and guidance, and of
peace and order depend. If the name of the commission's report was not taken, with the arrival of the commission
until the Day of Judgment, the name of the commission's report would not have been taken. With the arrival of the commission,
poor shopkeepers closed their shops to express a little sadness. In short, from the populated and
built areas, one of the crying shops had become a desolate place.
As far as we remember, Rai (Opinion) is the name of that power of thought which man in
individual and collective capacity gives high advice and counsel. And this is that power
that due to the wrong use of its remembrance, again becomes dependent on mad philosophy and
becomes a petitioner at the door of the pharmacy of unity. In individual circumstances, Rai (Opinion) then gains strength
when in the human brain complete balance and high clarity are present. In collective circumstances,
the correctness of opinion can then be realized when individuals are subject to a national assembly or a
regulated government is under accountability. In contrast, if in someone's status and in their brain, balance

at all. And no guidance can be found in such an opinion. And that opinion has no stability. Similarly,

7.right:
The wise sayings and words of Saad Zaghloul Pasha

Now, it is happening in the country that the news of the flood's emergence has spread, regarding this, I deemed it appropriate to translate a few wise words of the late Saad Zaghloul Pasha and present them to you, which are very good for the benefit of the field:-

1- Egypt wants complete independence, because this is the inherent right of nations. At this time, we are one reason, but the life of a nation cannot be disappointed by the fate of crushing another nation.

2- Being a humble subject of my nation seems very important to me. Claiming governance over it is a baseless attitude that is harmful.

3- A lover of freedom is not an extremist, because this is the benefit of love. A lover of slavery is an extremist, because slavery is the ultimate humiliation of the self.

4- The spirit of a nation is not something that disappears with laws, nor with oppression, nor with the great empires of the world.

5- O people of the country, it is the duty of all of you to specialize. Saad alone is not responsible; every person of the homeland is responsible.

6- If I say this, it is not my wish. It is not only that there is one nation in Egypt, and the Hindu is only the Egyptian nation.

7- Saad will always be alive even after death, because every Egyptian is Saad, rather, is more than Saad.

8- My life has no envy, no malice. In my soul, the national trust has not given way to anything.

9- Any place that claims to want freedom, has no minimal right.

that it is difficult to express one's thoughts well in Pashto.
The following writings are worth reading; readers
will realize for themselves to what extent
a subject can be expressed in Pashto. We praise
Sadrli Sahib's report and express our gratitude for his kindness.

(Editor)

The future of the Pashtun has been troubled from the beginning to the end. Today we tell the newspapers
of the world what this is, why there is such happiness; the Pashtun has expressed Pashto in his own way,
and to what extent can one write about the subject that came to the office
after the Pashtun's announcement. It seems as if people were eagerly
waiting for it, and this was a minute of the people that in the name of Pashtun, a heart-
attracting effect was present, from which the hearts of Pashtuns cannot be separated.
Today, in such a long period, it has achieved mind-boggling progress. Like this,
the cry of today's heart is rocking in the cradle; just by hearing the name, the desire is apparent.
It is not clear that in one day the Pashtun will be on every street. From this,
another thing is also known. That is, to what extent do the people of the south look for news
in other newspapers. Undoubtedly, the spiritual passion is a clear proof of
spiritual life. And may God make this national life of the spiritual grow day by day.

Pashtun

May 1928 AD
Past events are discussed based on principles; the success of today's seekers
lies in their own Pashto. And only those people are aware of this secret whose heart's eyes
are opened by acquiring Western knowledge. In short, self-preservation is a duty for everyone. If the Pashtuns also
or are considered a nation in Afghanistan. They too have every right to
fully protect themselves. In this regard, among other things, one point is also this
that they have started newspapers in their own language, which from time to time
express their thoughts. For this purpose, the Pashtun newspaper was started. For this, the honor of the people
should not be behind anyone else in the political, educational, etc., fields, and for their own
work, they should adopt the path that is sound and achieves the goal."
No pure Pashtun, if we start like this. "What is past is past. It is necessary to
keep the future in mind. This necessity has been felt by all developed
nations in their own time. It is a separate matter that today newspapers have become so numerous
that it is difficult to read them. But with the increase, there has been no
difference in that necessity. Walking around with a newspaper in hand at the time of
one's own purpose is not enough, and through the newspaper, every day at all times
one can talk to the readers. And it is a way to learn. The reason is
that the developed nations have been paying attention to this for hundreds of years. It would not be
bad if one casts a glance at the history of newspapers in other countries.
It is said that the true history of the newspaper started from Germany. In 1605, a
man named Emmanuel started a weekly newspaper by the name of Frank
Frankfurter Journal. Six years later (1611)
in London, a newspaper by the name of Weekly News was started.

May 1928
The sacrifice and welfare of free Afghanistan
In the past month of March, under the leadership of Lord Ronaldshay, the speech made at the East India Association meeting is worth reading so that the Pashtuns of Afghanistan may take a lesson from it. Even if a speech was made about King Amanullah Khan's great ambition and his efforts beyond his power for the progress and prosperity of his nation, and it is necessary to acknowledge his favor. This decision can be made well by the people of Afghanistan and the Naderi followers, Ali Ahmad Khan, and his patriotic followers, as to who truly wants freedom for Afghanistan. And if any thought has been expressed at the end, Lord Ronaldshay said at the beginning of his statement that it is not in the interest of Afghanistan to maintain a tense relationship with the British government. Rather, if it is true for the government of Great Britain that Afghanistan is between the government of Britain and the power of another, one should not forget another region that exists between Afghanistan and India, where there is no regular government of any kind. These are the things in their minds. Either education should be provided to them or they should be included with oneself. This is a matter that is entirely about them, that they should be included with oneself. Because where civilization and ignorance meet, it is necessary to remove the inclination toward ignorance there. -

The Pashtun reader can understand the purpose of my statement himself; the question of ignorance has been ruined by the wisdom of the predecessors. Now it is the turn of the poor Pashtuns. They will inform the people. At such a time, it is necessary for one to be with one's own. There is a saying that one cannot be sure of a kick from an old donkey.

Peshawar
Volume 5

Month of June 1928, corresponding to Dhu al-Hijjah 1346
The National Love of the King of Afghanistan
(By Maulana Abdul Hamid Afghan)

In Pashtun, this proverb is famous that the ear of the calf is known from the spring, and if someone looks closely at all the words and deeds of Amir Ghazi and looks at every aspect of his life, it becomes clear that God has created this man with a special nature and for a special task, and the love and affection he has in his heart for his nation is not something he has acquired, but rather God has created this quality in his nature. It is true that the ignorance and malice of the Pashtun is such that the love and affection that has been attained in him cannot be compared to anyone else. For the individuals whose hearts are devoted to the nation, and who are willing to destroy everything for the sake of the nation's profit and loss, how could they cause harm to that nation? This nation also belongs to the Amir, and he is well aware of it, yet he is madly in love with his nation and has reached the status of God in the Pashtun nation. From this, it is known that this national love is acquired.

Volume Major (Printed) Dhu al-Hijjah 1346
The National Love of the King of Afghanistan
(By Maulana Abdul Majeed Afghani)
In Pashto, this proverb is famous: "The ear does not get full from hearing."
If anyone contemplates all the words and deeds of the Ghazi Amir and looks at
every aspect of his life, it will become clearly evident to him
that God created this man with a special nature and for a special purpose,
and the love and affection that is in his heart for his nation
is not something he acquired, but rather God placed this quality in his
nature. It is not like the ignorance and malice of the Pashtuns,
for the love and affection that is acquired through effort
can be compared. The individuals of a nation who consider their own
blood brothers as enemies and waste their lives in the pursuit of selfish interests,
how can there be love for such a nation?
The Amir himself is from this nation and knows nothing else, yet even so,
he is madly in love with his nation and has reached the status of God-given
leadership among the Pashtuns. It is clear that he has attained this national love.

27.right:

is not, rather he has brought it with himself.

And this too was the love of truth, that upon his words
they dropped their weapons and pleaded that may your fortune and throne
remain established, we will not do this again.
This too was the effect of that love, that whenever I am in Jalalabad
taking leave of the border tribes to go to Europe, I always weep,
and this was the testimony that there is no greater shame in their
religion than weeping. And he said to them, why do you weep, because you
think that who will kill me, who will kill me.
Look, I am a man like you, and you are my strength; if I
die, then there are many other youths, another Pashtun will sit in my place
and will perform the same service, so do not look that you might lose your leader.
And no one can deny that anyone from the East who goes to
Europe, whether he is poor or a king, he recognizes everything of his homeland.
The life of Europe is such a fascination that whoever goes there
once, then everything else is left behind. Many Indians
are in Europe; the Iranian dandy is also in Paris in a perfume shop
.

The self-forgetful Natak did such a thing that in the world, up

Saadullah Khan, son of Captain Khan Sahib, wrote a letter from abroad.
He writes:-
That when Amir Sahib came to visit the rifle factory in Birmingham,
then we three Pashtuns also went to welcome him. At the time in the foreign country,
at the station, only very special people were allowed, but we three
were also given permission.
We went to the station when there was a commotion and it became known that Amir
Sahib was coming. In the meantime, he arrived, and this was the first day that he
came within five meters of us and stopped at the train.
When the train started to move, he called out in a loud voice to the young men,
saying "Long live the King of Pashtuns." Hearing this, Amir Sahib put his head out of the train.
outside.

I: May I be sacrificed for you, we are Pashtuns.
Amir Sahib: He quickly reached out his hand and shook hands with sincerity.
did.

I: Engineering.
Amir Sahib: Come to Kabul.
I: May I be sacrificed for you, it is a good thought, we also left the homeland with this intention.
We have come here.
Amir Sahib, how many more Pashtun youths are there in the country?
Sacrifice-
It is in this that there is love and affection in the heart of a human being, that there is love for one's own nation in one's essence, and that one has found the pain of one's country in its welfare; such a person will never forget their nation in any circumstance, nor will they be forgotten, and these people are certainly successful in the world, and their every work and action will not go to waste.
Thus, the love of his nation is in the nature of Amir Ghazi, and the wheat-colored life of Europe will never make him oblivious to the interests of his nation.
It is hoped from God that he will either sacrifice himself for the progress of the nation or will fulfill his vision.
Or I will lose myself after him.
Or I will bring back the drowned friend.

Tax and the harvest season

The will of God and the will of man are not the same. Man's imagination
is riding a horse, while God's is built upon reality.
As much as human thought goes, it was clear that this year
there would be grain in the storehouses. The abundance and growth of the crops, on the surface,
was a message of joy for the poor. For many, it was a reason
to be at ease, a reason for the sustenance of life and the relief of hunger,
had begun. But this was not God's will. With the ripening of the crop, suddenly
the same people who were destined for it had reached it. With the ripening of the crop, suddenly
all joy turned into sorrow. The poor landowners are not only
sad that all their hard work and the hope for profit
did not come to fruition. They have more grief and worry about the fact that
how will the tax be paid. There is no doubt that tax is
a narrow grave. It does not look at the back or the end, but still, mercy and justice
are remembered as good in this world; mercy on the condition of the poor is necessary.
On the poverty of the destitute, the helplessness of the laborers, the hardship of the landowners, and again
on such a barren harvest year, consideration is necessary. In truth, no
landowner will be happy with it. The government should, in a fair manner,
determine the year, whether the crops were destroyed or empty. If it is truly empty
then it is necessary that the government does not take tax because the crop did not grow.
Then from which side should the tax come? For the small-time or other landowners, goods are not sold for tax
and if they are sold, then the responsibility will fall on the clerk. In Punjab
there is also this difficulty that whatever kindness the government has shown, for that kindness of mine
therefore the landowners have every hope. It is hoped that mercy will be shown on their condition.

32.right:
June 1928
16
Pashtun
Zamindar Association Mardan
My dear! Peace be upon you
With a kind look, please print these few lines in the Pashtun magazine
for the information of the common landlords. We will be grateful.
Due to the difficulties felt by the landlord people residing in Mardan,
in order to find ways to get rid of these difficulties and for progress and improvement,
on the 10th of May 1928, in a public meeting, an association of landlord
people named "Zamindar Association" was established. The president of the meeting,
Khan Sahib Sarfaraz Khan Sahib of Mardan, was elected. The purpose of the association
is this:
A. To maintain good relations among the landlords and to deal with the
effects that the landlord has a connection with, and to advocate for the
landlords' interests.
B. To find ways for the progress of landlords, the benefit of agriculture, and
the reform of agriculture under government law, and to
strive for those ways.
Your well-wisher, Mohammad Akbar, Secretary, Zamindar Association Mardan
Note:
It would look better if instead of "Association", a self-
Pashto word is used. Whatever it may be.
(Editor)

Nowadays, in the Frontier Province, a special unrest between the educated class and the Khans
is ongoing. The real issue is not known, and even if it were known, it cannot be
spoken of much. Outwardly, it appears that in their view, the government is
working for interests. The educated want that in the name of reforms, they should
be strictly bound, and then the Khans will not be able to do anything because they are
The matter is very complicated.
The Khans say that we will bind them within the Sharia, so that
no one other than the Mullah can approach them, and the Mullahs are in their hands because
we give Zakat, we give Isqat, and these poor students are at the doors
crying out, "Bring the stipend, where is your blood?" The educated, however,
call them "looters of the faith," and if an educated person dies, then
there is no Fatiha or Durood; the Mullahs have issued a fatwa of apostasy.
It is just that once the government approves the Sharia, then what is there? Everything
is theirs, come what may. Then there will be no lawyers, no clients; everything

they say, those decisions will be carried out, and the Mullah Sahib will
put his seal on them. And then, in the elections, there is a strong hope that instead of
the pious Khan, someone from Mardan or Radio or Takal, a man of status,
will be there.
Now the question is, if the Khans want Sharia from the government, then this is apparent
It is a matter that it will be related to the transactions, so if the government asks them

33.right:
Pashtun - June 1928 - 18
I have already left these two or three matters of etiquette and morals with you. Whether you acted upon them, or merely claimed to, or if a person filed a lawsuit of a hundred thousand against some Khan, by which Sharia would the Mullah Sahib settle it—now we understand what answer the Khan Sahibs will give. And if the government, in the service of the educated, has had you act upon those, now you want more—you cannot even manage your own "bar room." Always there are stories of those Muslims in the disputes; setting your own fee feels heavy to you. One lawyer asks for ten rupees, then another comes to you and sends a clerk. "It is not possible, I will do all your work for five rupees"—and in this matter, there are three that you will see. You cannot even manage the correctness of a scribe, you cannot manage the state of an Islamia school, there are counted nets, you cannot manage their endowments, so how will you manage the entire province? You have not even given this much, and that many Muslims eat from their own chests, and if you were to give something, you yourselves know what you would do with it then.
Now I am wondering what answer the educated friends will give to these questions. We are also busy watching the spectacle; we are not hopeless in the Khans nor in the educated. This war is also a festival. If the Khans win the field, we will make the Khan a Mullah, and if the educated win, then we will enter among the Laili-ran; the name of the nation also comes to us. Idle talk is no achievement.

The summary outline of pure Islam that the Holy Quran has shown us. This is the witness to the true nature (Fitrat Allah) upon which He created people. The true recognition of Islam is the qualities of the questioner—this is the result of Islamic education—and this is the intent. This is the true nature; it is the parents who, by the hands of other nations, raise them under the banner of Judaism, Christianity, or Magianism, and because of this, their natural qualities of bravery, manliness, and justice are changed into other traits. They remain fearful and helpless in demanding their necessary and moral rights. The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gives this example: just as a healthy child is born from a healthy mother, who is not missing an ear, nor a head—it is completely perfect—but some cut off an ear, some a nose, and some something else; you are born with this offspring just as they are born from the nations of the world.
There is no difference in the birth of both, but you yourselves cut off the head and face of your children—bad shape, bad deeds—a nation takes the upbringing of itself and its children into its own hands, for the sake of obtaining their common national benefits, it is a rebellious provision—according to Islamic education, this faith and belief is the questioner for every person.

35.right:
June 1920

The leaders' sermon (continued from previous page)
I seek refuge in Allah, the All-Hearing, the All-Knowing, from the accursed Satan.
In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful.
Praise be to Allah, the Exalted, the Magnificent, the Warner, and blessings and peace be upon His Messenger, who was sent to the black and the red (all mankind). As for what follows: O dear Islam and nation, the leadership that you have entrusted to me, although I do not see this capability in myself, for leadership, a being is needed who is very, very trustworthy, and I have so many weaknesses that no one else has. However, since you have placed your trust in me, I accept your reliance upon me, and I pray to God Almighty that He, through His own doing, makes this humble advice beneficial for you. And I hope from you that if there is any flaw in me, you will inform me and correct it.

A view of the current situation: Dear ones and elders, today in the world, our nation's current time is being viewed with a critical eye for division. In the field of knowledge, we are behind everyone; we are setting aside crafts and prosperity, and in the field of trade, it appears as a shameful act. The point is that even today we are guilty, because among us... [text repeats/corrupt]... it is a matter of complete deficiency that a human or a nation itself is not complete in this. For us to progress in this world, we must find this spirit within ourselves, and for this, the Lord of the Worlds has taught us the lesson of sacrifice in His Book, verse: "Indeed, Allah has purchased from the believers their lives and their properties [in exchange] for that they will have Paradise." Allah Almighty has purchased from the Muslims their lives and their wealth, and in exchange for that...
35.left:

Pashtun
is one who keeps his homeland pure and safe from the influence of foreign nations, and the spirit of sacrifice for his homeland is always present in their hearts. This is one reason for true success. In the Ghazwa of Fiqh-e-Makkah, when the command came from the Almighty for the Muslims, especially for the Muhajireen, it came specifically in this manner: "Expel them from where they expelled you." And from the homes and lands from which you were driven out, and your civil and financial rights were usurped, you must also purify those very homes and lands from the influence of foreign nations. The political articles that the Holy Prophet (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him and his family) has guided us toward through prophecy indicate that the division of Muslim territories would occur. By distancing themselves from them, they became like straw, and their awe and fear in the eyes of other nations would be due to their own actions. Apart from this, seeking other remedies and creating other forms and transgressions is misleading the nation. The entire essence of this subject is contained in this Hadith:

And from Thawban (may Allah be pleased with him), he said: The Messenger of Allah (peace and blessings of Allah be upon him) said: "The nations are about to call one another against you as people call one another to eat from a dish." A person asked: "Is it because we will be few in number that day?" He said: "No, you will be many, but you will be like the foam of a torrent. And Allah will surely remove the fear of you from the hearts of your enemies, and He will cast 'al-wahn' into your hearts." It was asked: "What is 'al-wahn', O Messenger of Allah?" He said: "Love of the world and hatred of death."

Meaning, those things that have ruined your blessings and rights are the love of the world and the dislike of death. This is the reason why the Indian movement, at the very time of its progress, faces rebellion. Because one who is accustomed to the luxurious dining table of the wealthy can never be patient, and we were not prepared for the difficult stages. If we choose Islamic politics among the politicians, then in our hearts, we will be at ease. And finally, we say: All praise is due to Allah, Lord of the Worlds. Only.

39.right:
June 1920

Hazrat Omar (RA) and the custom of the Nile River
(Ahi Abdul Ghafoor)

The custom was established that every year if a virgin was not thrown (into the river), the river would dry up and there would be a great famine in Egypt. So Amr ibn al-Aas (RA) was astonished. So he wrote the incident to Hazrat Omar (RA). So he wrote a few words in reply and commanded that these words be thrown into the river. Those words are:

In the name of Allah, the Most Gracious, the Most Merciful. To the Nile of Egypt, from Abdullah Omar ibn al-Khattab. Thereafter, if you flow by yourself, then we have no need of you, and if you flow by Allah, then flow in the name of Allah. I begin in the name of God, who is the Most Gracious and Most Merciful, to the Nile River of Egypt, from Abdullah, whose name is Omar ibn al-Khattab. After that, the meaning is that if you flow by your own power, then there is no need for you, and if you flow by the command of God Almighty, then flow in the name of God.

So when those words were written, Amr ibn al-Aas (RA) threw them into the river, and the river's water rose. And it has not risen beyond that limit since.

Allahu Akbar. This is the result of people with pure beliefs, how much honor the Pure Lord has bestowed upon them.
O Allah, guide all Muslims, especially Pashtuns, and protect our traditions from being swallowed up. - Amen -

A traveler's hour

I wrote this ghazal in London on May 24, 1924. On that day,
the gardens were full of people. I also went out with a pure heart, with my brothers.
It seemed as if you would say that it was Eid for them. At that time, I
remembered my home, village, and beautiful fields, and the misery of my brothers came to my heart.
I became sad and went to a place, I fell into an effort, and in a state of thought,
these words flowed from my tongue:-

May no more sorrow remain in my heart - may my whole being become a burning coal.
My heart does not want to go to the homeland again - I have become old, may it be torn to pieces.
When I think about the condition of my brothers - my whole body trembles in fear.
I will be imprisoned in chains once again - if the desolate homeland appears before my eyes.
I beg Zargai - why should I be a wanderer of my own homeland?
When a human being is tied like an animal - every friend should stay away from here.
When other nations of the world celebrate - my own homeland, alas, becomes a spectacle for them.
I do not know which sorrow to scatter - wherever I go, there is a sign of it.
I have spent my whole life in the wilderness - why are there cries after me everywhere?
I am weeping for the plants of my homeland - I have no other choice but this.
Either the heart will be cut from the feet before
Or the black soil will be piled upon me

Announcement

Regarding the announcement of a (50) rupee prize by Abdul Rahman Sahib for the publication of (Pashtun), the life history page should not be less than the specified page and not more than the Pashto standard.
A clear notification of the subject is necessary. The decision will be made by a panel of judges. That is, (15) complete copies must reach the office. The result will be published in the next month's Pashtun.

A request from Mian Ahmad Shah, Editor of Pashtun

A few words with article writers
(1) Articles should be in clear, simple Pashto.
(2) The words of the article should be in clear, pure Pashto. Market slang and vulgar language
are not needed.
(3) Special consideration for religion, ethics, and civilization is necessary.
(4) Articles that promote bad habits are not needed.
(5) Stories that are not shameful and do not contain bad habits
should be written.
(6) For the style of writing, learn from Pashtun.
(7) Consideration of brevity is necessary as much as possible.
(8) The article must be written on one side of the paper.
(9) The article must reach us by the tenth of every month.
(10) If an article is not published, it will not be returned.
(11) The editor has the full authority to accept or reject the submitted article.

It is said that in Afghanistan, regarding the news circulating about the people of Hindustan, some claim it is largely unfounded. They say that the Muslims of Hindustan, in light of what has transpired in Afghanistan, are being portrayed in a manner that is deeply misleading. We understand that the people of Hindustan, under the oppression of their governments at home, do not fully grasp how their affairs are viewed in Afghanistan. There, they are treated with great kindness, particularly by those striving for the freedom of Hindustan. The news circulating about the people of Afghanistan, in truth, is for those seeking liberation for the Muslims of Hindustan. It is vitally important that the people of Hindustan be treated with kindness in Afghanistan, so that they may pursue their aspirations for independence. We understand that this news is, in essence, for those in Hindustan who are working towards freedom. It is not unfounded that the people of Hindustan seek independence in Afghanistan.

which has greatly increased the courage and determination of the workers and members.

of presenting to you the entire proceedings, income and expenditure, remaining balance, and educational status of the past year.

who possess the quality of bravery and a central point, meaning gathering around one leader.

Pharaoh had destroyed all the rights, honor, and respect of the Children of Israel. The Prophet of Islam, Hazrat

with us are the Children of Israel." That is, my first demand from you is the freedom of the Children of Israel. Remove the bonds of captivity from them.
Return to them the rights that you have taken from them.
This general outline of Islam that the Quran shows us is the true nature.
"The nature of Allah upon which He created mankind" is a witness to this. All humans possess original innate qualities.
This is the result of Islamic teachings, and this is the intention. However, whenever the natural
dispositions of a nation cannot remain correct on their own, due to this, the need for Islamic education arose.
When the original dispositions of a nation are corrupted and their natural rights are lost,
the reason for this is wrong upbringing, meaning their living under the shadow of a foreign nation.
The Holy Prophet (PBUH) has stated in his blessed words: "There is no child born but
upon the natural disposition, then his parents make him a Jew, or a Christian, or a Magian, just as an animal gives birth to a complete animal. Do you
find among them any with a cut ear, until you yourselves cut it?" Every child is born free from its mother.
There is no yoke of slavery of any power around his neck. This is "true nature." But
if parents raise him at the hands of a foreign nation, under the influence of Judaism and Christianity,
or Magianism, due to this, their natural qualities
of bravery, manliness, and justice are changed into bad qualities. He becomes fearful and helpless in demanding his own necessary and
natural rights. The Holy Prophet (PBUH) gave a clear example, saying
that a healthy calf is born from a cow or buffalo, whose ear is not missing, nor its nose,

65.right:
January 1929

In this meeting, with the participation of Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, Abdul Akbar Khan, Rustam Khan, and other Pashtuns, this Jirga began in the month of November. In this meeting, it was decided to form the Pashtun "Zalmo Jirga" (Youth Jirga), similar to the "All India Youth League" group in India. The basic purpose of forming this Jirga was to show the Pashtuns the path of national unity, independence, and the struggle for their rights. In this meeting, the Pashtun leaders emphasized in their speeches that Pashtuns must adopt the path of unity and strive for a common goal. The leaders of this Jirga told the participants of the meeting that without unity, Pashtuns cannot achieve their rights and for this purpose, they must support each other in all circumstances. Likewise, decisions were also made in this meeting to strengthen the cultural and social affairs of the Pashtuns. It is worth mentioning that the message of unity was spread in the meetings of this Jirga and it guided the Pashtuns to rise as a united force. In the course of the work of this Jirga, the need for increased efforts for unity and consensus among the youth in different regions of Pashtunkhwa was mentioned. ---

70.right:
January 1920

I say amen to this statement of my Pashtun brothers of the American nation, who give a lot of money in the path of noble service and love!

The truth is that today, work cannot be done without money. You see, today, any nation that does not have a strong treasury cannot have new propaganda, hospitals, and schools for its nation, and cannot serve the country and religion of its nation; and if a nation does not have a treasury, then you see that it is equal to nothing else, which is the state of the nation.

If a person asks why Pashtuns do not do as other nations do, this statement is wrong; Pashtuns give much more charity than their means. But their method of charity is such that it is not of immediate benefit to the nation, it is not eternal; undoubtedly, their method of charity is in the form of the past era, but today its benefit is a loss to the nation.

The era has changed; it was necessary for Muslims to have changed as well. But they did not change with the era, because instead of benefit, it is a loss for them. In the past, people would serve the nation and religion for free, and you would offer money for people, and they would give it to us; in this way, the service of the nation continued, and the servants were also able to make a living.

But you see today that everything is for money. The era has changed; propaganda, publication, school, imamate—in fact, there is no matter that can be done without money, and such a person will not die who girds his loins to serve the nation for the sake of God. If a person girds his loins, then if he is wealthy, like our Pashtuns, he will be able to make a living, but if he is poor, then what?

For this reason, today it is necessary for Muslims to move with the pace of the era, for Pashtuns to form a national treasury with unity like other nations for money, goods, and cash ideas, and to help the nation, and to remove the neediness of the nation, and to support the servants of Islam. Because I hope that our Pashtun brothers will imitate the Pashtun brothers of America and will walk in their footsteps.

The day that our American brothers' charity and alms were placed by the association for the education of the group, because today the most necessary thing for us is to acquire knowledge, the receipt is below:

78.right:
Pashtun
Idle Talk
(Maulana Abdul Majeed - Afghani)
In the previous issue of Pashtun, there were some disappointed idle remarks regarding the Khans and the educated people of the Frontier Province, and my meaning was that if they also pay attention to this side, a lot of work will be done, because at this time, this educated class is the heart and mind for the nation, and the reform of these is the reform of the whole nation. My meaning is not that there are no good friends in this group, as their love is also known.
Look at how much you talk to us, you talk about full benefits, you talk like parrots, and think that among you there are also those who have belittled us to the point of humiliation, and are ready to go.
And now you judge whether we get upset by your true criticisms, while you get upset by our idle talk.
It is true that the powerful strike and do not even let the weak speak.
I am very regretful now that why I made such idle remarks that my friends got upset; I will be careful again and will not make idle remarks.
It is true that speaking the truth is necessary; idle talk is just idle talk.
I ask for your forgiveness, and as a punishment for these idle remarks, yours
I am absolutely ready to hear the truth, and many will have spoken the truth, remember that, and if the Khan Sahibs have been offended, then I ask for their forgiveness too, because I am very fond of them.
I am writing this in a burning state.

(Mian Ahmad Shah, B.A. Barrister, Peshawar)
Welcome, may your arrival be blessed. Your safe return is not only a cause of history and pride for you, nor is it only a source of joy and happiness for Afghanistan, but all Pashtuns, whether here or far, whether in the mountains or the plains, whether at home or abroad, are blooming with joy. With one voice, we say welcome to you, may you not be weary, this arrival is the festival of festivals and the joy of joys. Whenever the people were worried about you, it was because the Pashtuns and the leaders were their guardians, and you yourself

of the home. Nation-building, which some consider a form of self-centeredness and making others laugh at oneself.

Welcome to the London Pashto students
to the King of Afghanistan

This is the welcome sermon of the London Pashto students who are receiving education in short, which we present in the service of King Amanullah Khan. It is not the reason that we consider the young men prepared for Urdu happy in the service of their King, it was a matter of longing and unfulfilled desire to see those imaginary thoughts, but these are matters of fate. God and His will are not efforts, so that sermon has been sent to be printed in Pashto, that King Amanullah Khan will be happy and will be very pleased, it is as follows:-
In the service of the Pashto King, may the Sultanate of Afghanistan always remain,

In the homeland, in the name of Aman, may it be ahead,
May the Pashtunabad of Punjab not be from Zahrat,
May the Kings make the Pashtun their own in their entirety,

In the world, may the great Khan of Pashto be remembered,
In this turn, may a young man be remembered at the head,
It is not that he is remembered only in beauty (King),

We tell them: We are from the dear land of Pashtunkhwa, England, and the sky is witness that even with knowledge and effort, the Pashtun nation has appeared today in the world like a new sun without doubt. In Pashto, a new life will...

The Pashtun's educational clamor
A few words about Pashto
Dear sisters! Today there is a clamor that Pashto should be educated. This is something to be accepted. But this is known through patience; what is happening to our guides? It is necessary that we walk step by step. I do not say that if you also use your feet in education and use your intellect, then higher education will be in your sight and your future life will pass in happiness. Do not ask from anyone and do not spend your time in sleep, tea, and luxuries. Pashtuns are ruined, so have mercy on the poor travelers and treat them with domestic hard work. Do not spend your time until twelve o'clock at night and spend the day until two or three o'clock cooking tea and gossiping. Do not spend your time in fights between mothers-in-law, daughters-in-law, and husbands. I present this request to these sisters.

Another point is that whether they are happy or sad, if someone tells them about the cleanliness and beauty of the house, they reply that they are busy. If it is a girl, she says that my parents are busy with working sisters, which work should I do? There is a lot of work and many children, and if she is a wife, she says that the mother-in-law has made peace with the husband, and if I do a lot of work, they are not happy. These are the words of uneducated women; they are always complaining about parents, mothers-in-law, daughters-in-law, and domestic chores.

Song of Freedom
What is sweeter than freedom in the world? Whoever sees it, whoever watches it,
From their mouth comes this one cry: O Freedom! O Freedom! For your sake, one hides their own self.
Freedom! Where are you? Which is your house? Which place is your homeland? Have I
looked for you anywhere? Is your house above the waves of the sea? Or in an airplane
flying with someone? Or are you a lightning bolt on the peaks of the mountains? Or are you
hidden in the world of the stars? Or are you sitting in the mouth of a thirty-maund cannon?
Or are you under the deck of ships? Or are you sleeping with pearls
at the bottom of the sea? Finally, tell me, where are you? O Freedom! Your grief has made me
lose my mind; my intellect does not know where you are. O Freedom! I am wandering
for you. You are the light of my eyes, a piece of my liver, a drop of my heart.
(2) Every province of India, every city, every street, every alley, every
house, I have searched for you, but you were not in them. The 320 million people of India,
young, old, drinking, not drinking, bearded, big-mustached, without mustaches, poor, rich,
I asked them all separately, but no one showed you to me.
(3) The high mountains of India, your such peaks, your such strong
canyons, your such large caves, so many groups and gatherings,
who found you, that now you are weeping for them with a white head?
The beautiful land of India, the fertile land, the vast land, the young land,

The way to achieve the goal

In the present era, the state of Pashto is pitiable. Although in the past it did not progress like Egypt or Iran, it was not behind the knowledge of the scholars of its time compared to other nations until the twelfth century. Mahmud Ghaznavi, Ahmad Shah, or Sher Shah Suri were not ignorant people, but rather lovers of knowledge. They knew the pen. If one looks at history, it will become clear that in that era, until the later centuries, the British or other Western nations had not made as much progress as Sher Shah had given to India. In that era, Pashto intellect was in its place. Spiritual life, understanding meaning, and the works of Pashto were respected, and for the dishonorable, death was preferred over having much wealth.

But look at this era, where losing faith is considered an honor. Knowledge has been made a means of earning money; they consider the current state correct and according to their own wishes. Many will say that God is pleased with this. They would not consider life worth living for the sake of dishonor. They will not spend a single penny for the nation, but they call the nationalist a fool and an idiot—they will laugh at him.

Every person says that we were in a good state in that history. Now we have fallen into the well of decline. But this is an obvious matter that it is not a miracle. Rather, it is a harmful thing. Come, find out the cause of these reasons and then find a way to remove those causes.

There are two major reasons for the decline of a nation:

Alas, that we, the Pashtun nation, have limited the use of these holy verses only to formally expressing grief on the occasion of deaths.
And we have become very distant from its real purpose, having forgotten it.
The real purpose of the verse was to make it clear to Muslims that for a nation, sacrificing oneself in the way of Allah is nothing to be avoided, and no kind of worldly, personal, or financial loss, which is the inevitable result of every national struggle, should prevent a true Muslim from continuing towards his goal. Instead, rather than learning a lesson of sacrifice from the holy verses, the Pashtun nation recites these verses without understanding their meaning only when a person dies, from which remembrance of death or life, no harm or benefit reaches the nation. Servants and lights were extinguished. And they do not let me. Their negligence

they were destroyed themselves. And they are still being destroyed.

In 1890, the Iranian government's granting of the tobacco monopoly to a British company created a very wide field for Sayyid Sahib. To take advantage of this time, Sayyid Sahib sent a famous letter to the supreme Mujtahid of the time, Mirza Hassan Shirazi, informing him of the creation of a document against the Iranian government and the waste and extravagance of the common people's wealth for the benefit of the enemies of Islam. In that letter, he wrote: That until now, the profits made on the lines have confirmed the right to increase in the economy. And granting the monopoly of tobacco has surrendered all of Iran to the hands of Europe. This letter of Sayyid Sahib, which was sent to the clerics, had a great impact.
---
When we look, the Iranian government did not observe any respect for that famous and holy figure. And they would forcibly remove Sayyid Sahib from the shrine. The Iranian minister, Ali Asghar Khan, showed great hostility toward those residing in the shrine of Shah Abdul Azim. And with a web of lies, [he claimed] that an Armenian was kept as a partner in this great and holy shrine, and that he was causing a disturbance in the sanctity of this place. Here, by the Armenian, he meant the one whose name is Jamal al-Din al-Afghani. There was no one else except that Armenian.
The residents did not believe it at first, and they were also struck with fear, but Ali Asghar Khan insisted. And he gave advice for an honest inspection. It is a clear matter that such an inspection is impossible in a holy shrine. When they brought him out, they saw neither an Armenian nor anyone else—(not uncircumcised)—but returning to the shrine became impossible. They brought out the guards who had been hiding behind the two.

Due to the great deal of trouble caused by the situation in Iran, Sayyid Sahib strongly opposed Iran in London through articles and speeches. In reality, Sayyid Sahib's supporters in Iran and abroad intensified their activities because of that situation. In any case, the Iranian government had confronted Sayyid Sahib in that matter. Respected Editor, Pashtun, Peace be upon you.
In the July issue, I wrote a poem for the first poetry gathering,

May India be the fate of the people of India

120.right:
Pashtun
Volume 2
June 1939
Bacha Khan's Speech
Dear and respected ones! You are aware of the state of our homeland due to the British colonial system. They have seized our wealth, and our people live in poverty and misery. My heart's desire is that we must become united and struggle against this oppression. They are trying by all means to sow discord among us. But we must understand that our benefit lies in unity. If we do not unite, they will continue to play with our destiny. Today, I call upon you to come and join hands for the freedom of our homeland. We have made many sacrifices in the past years, but these sacrifices are only valuable if we reach our goal. For this, we must believe in ourselves and stand up for our rights. This struggle is not easy, but if we remain steadfast on the path of truth, then success is certain.

Regarding service to Pashtun:
Those who have knowledge of the mentioned field, it is necessary for them to enlighten others about it as well. Just as we have made efforts in the past days to prevent the enemy, it is necessary to carry out our actions for the prosperity of the homeland. Today, our homeland needs every individual to work according to their ability for progress. The measures taken by the British government in Pashtunkhwa are meant to deprive the people of their rights. We must protect ourselves from such conspiracies and become one hand for our national interests.

The owner of that field is He,
The owner of that field is He,
The creation is His remembrance,
The people are His remembrance,
The world is His earth,
The world is His earth.
(During the time of Fazal Rahim Sahib)

Those who have the knowledge mentioned in the field, it is useful for them to inform others about it. Today, for the development of our country, each person needs to work according to his ability. What actions have been taken by the British government in Pakhtunkhwa, they are to destroy the rights of the people. Regarding the true civilization and education, it is a great necessity for the well-being of the Pashtun and the Pashtun himself that steps be taken to establish a system at the national level so that a broad and standard education system can be created for the people of the country. This is a path in which every citizen of the country can obtain their right. For the importance of this education, it is necessary that efforts be made to create a curriculum at the national level that will enhance the mental abilities of the people. Its goal should be the promotion of knowledge and wisdom for the progress of the homeland, not just for a specific class. The current system that exists pays attention to the interests of a specific class, due to which many members of our society are left without education. We must try to ensure that education is in accordance with the needs of the people and that every citizen can benefit from the blessing of education in their own country. No matter how many obstacles there are in this path, we must move forward for a bright future and work for the progress of our country in the light of knowledge. Our goal should not just be building schools, but creating an environment in which everyone has an equal opportunity to learn. With this, every individual in our society will participate in the progress of the country and a strong society will be created.
127.left:
A short note on the great need for the development of true civilization and education:

In reality, national education is an essential step for the common interests of all and the well-being of society. Unfortunately, the current system in the field of education has a series of shortcomings, due to which the people of the country remain deprived of their true right. In these circumstances, a major change is needed in the field of education that opens the doors of knowledge for every citizen of the country. Current education is for a limited circle, while education as a social right should reach everyone. For the expansion of education, it is necessary that policies be formulated at the government level that take into account the needs of the people and that practical steps be taken for the progress of the country. In this way, the people of our society will not only be equipped with the light of knowledge, but will shine in the world as a strong and developed society. It is the duty of every Pashtun to strive for the popularization of education and to raise their voice for this important cause.

Flattery is a disgrace

Flattery is a very useless and bad act. Its meaning
is that a person, against the permission of his conscience, will
express unmerited praise and it is a clear matter
that one who praises unmeritedly can lie to himself.
And lying is such a hideous thing that in the Quran
a curse has been placed upon the liars. How shameful it is
that a person does not possess a quality and we, for the sake of
him, turn it into praise. One harm is that the flatterer
falls under the curse of God upon the liars, and secondly, the one
who is being flattered, a kind of pride and arrogance is born
in him. So, whatever qualities he had, they also vanish

153.right:
Pashtun Elementary

A look at Albania

Albania's (late) King "Ahmet Zogu" is one of those figures of the world who rose above the ordinary status of his merit and courage.

This king's story is the story of the death of Jalal Beg, among the tribes of Albania. Besides the status of his own business, he was the trusted deputy of Sultan Abdul Hamid. He was born in 1895. How Ahmet Zogu was raised in Albania is a story in itself. Until 1915, Albania was part of the Turkish government. But when a freedom movement started against the Sultan's faulty government in Turkey, the idea of freedom for his own country began in Ahmet Zogu's heart. At the age of twenty, he joined the movement. He endured exile, imprisonment, and the hardships of war. Finally, he succeeded in his goal. In 1925, he became the Prime Minister of Albania and in 1925, he became the head of Albania. Finally, in 1928, due to his ability and power, he was made King of Albania, and he took the title "Zogu".

During the time of the freedom movement, he faced many enemies. The people of Albania, like the Wazirs, believed in blood feuds. Controlling these bloodthirsty people was not an easy task. Ahmet Zogu was a brave man. He controlled them with an iron hand. He was targeted by enemies. There were reports of three hundred (300) attempts to assassinate him. Many attempts were made on his life, but fate saved him, and he remained alive.

He kept his palace under the protection of machine guns and often did not participate in public meetings.

Ahmet Zogu was a very learned man. He could speak German, French, Turkish, and Serbian. He was fond of studying history and spent his lonely nights busy with work for the betterment of the country.

Zogu was very skilled in maintaining law and order among the people. He brought his enemies under control through his good behavior. Whenever he found out that someone intended to rebel, he would invite them to the palace and bring them under control through his good behavior and sweet speech.

A priest, Fan Noli, was his very staunch enemy. When Zogu came to power, he exiled him. He lived in America for a long time. Finally, he became seriously ill, and for his treatment, financial ---

The homeland cannot be changed. They are who they are. They are the green lions of the mountains. Bravery and the sword are the birthright of every Pashtun. And this is because the Khan of Khans, and for the sake of honor and protection, bravery and the sword are essential components. We have examined all the geographical, national, political, and ethnic conditions of the Pashtun. This nation's life is tied to bravery and the sword. Khushal Khan Khattak spent his whole life writing about Pashto and the sentiments of their courage, which is known to everyone, and it is the identity of the Pashtun. If the Khan is ruined, fighting with the sword is national honor, national progress, and the identity of the nation's prosperity. It is the true voice of the Pashtun. Following in the footsteps of Sher Shah Suri, the Pashtun has remained free from the slavery of the Mughals. From this brief statement, it must have become clear to you what Pashto is and why we call Khushal Khan Khattak a source of pride and an authentic Pashtun. Khushal Khan's poetry, patriotism, and personality:-
Patriotism is poetry, and poetry is patriotism. In these two, the spirit of Afghan patriotism is woven into poetry in such a way that every flower seems appropriate for the country's meadows. Wherever Khushal Khan's poetry is discussed, this patriotism is also discussed on appropriate occasions.
Khushal's war poetry - its usage, rhythm, and rhyme stir the heart of every Pashtun. And it stands as a monument over the history of Pashto. These poets wrote that the war of a Pashtun against a Pashtun and the hope of a Pashtun war can be seen. And the thoughts of his wars are the judges. In these great volumes of the era, the theory is that the merits and beauties of these poets are all present. It is a true mirror of Pashtun patriotic poetry. Because it is a reflection of the Pashtun's simple, sharp, and warrior nature and their national state. In this Diwan, the hardships of the Pashtun's life are depicted in every home. In every village, the Pashtun war, their internal conflicts, the oppression of the Khans, the expansion of oil, the instilling of fear, the Pashtun's battles with the Mughals, the destruction of the Pashtun land, the habits and customs of the people, their way of life, bravery, hospitality, and the standards of other civilization and culture, and the identity of the Afghan, moral standards, and warfare.

Poetic descriptions of music. Poetic study of natural laughter. Ghazals of love and affection. Rubaiyat and qita'at in chronograms, etc., poets and myself in this style. January 1929

Women and Service to the Nation

I have had this thought for a long time that
in the service of our Pashtun nation, those women
who have put their lives on the line for the survival of the nation
and have made a great struggle for the survival of the nation.
Many stories of women are present in history
but I will work with brevity. And I will only mention
some of those brave women who in this era
have done great deeds. Rather, if I were to
describe it, then it is highly certain that people would say
that in the past, the era was different.

My purpose from this is only that I remove this wrong idea
from the minds of the nation that women are only
created for carnal desires. And that their
entire upbringing is not necessary. And that their creator
has no other responsibilities upon them regarding the
necessities that are prevalent in the present time. If
it is not, then it will become clear to you that women
are not for fulfilling carnal desires. Rather, upon them
the service of the nation in both 'war' and 'peace' times
is very heavy. If a woman is cowardly, her children
is a coward. If a woman is not herself aware of the secret
of sacrificing in the path of the nation, then she will never [teach] her children
163.left:
January 1929

to be prepared for sacrificing in the path of the nation and the country.
In this article, you will be shown about the
hard times that came upon the Turks. Constantinople was under the
occupation of the Allies (British, French, Italian). The servants of the nation
were standing ready like a wall. It was a time of trial.
A time like that, his name was also in the list
included. From the enemy's side, for his arrest,
thousands of rupees reward was set. He, at that
time, entrusted his children to strangers. And with his head in his hands,
he takes the path of the English. And he joins the enemy's army
for recruitment. And you will be shown such a
mother. That at that time when
her sons were hungry, and the situation was ongoing,
she says to them, 'Oh children! My desire is still unfulfilled.
I will see that blessed day when you
sacrifice your heads in the path of God'. And then she says to them,
'I will not consider you my own children if you
commit disloyalty to your country and nation'.
In this same way, you will come to know that women
can perform much greater service to the country and religion.
But from this article, my intention is not
to mean that I want that so-called civilization, education, and rights
for which are prevalent in the West.
(Europe)

165.left:
Pashtun Utmanzai
21 June 1939
Volume (5) Number (14)
A message of condolence from Pashtun
The struggle of Khan Sahib Khudai Dad Khan for the service of the nation is an example that will always be a torch for future generations. He spent a large part of his life for the unity of the Pashtuns and for winning their rights. In his death, not only his family but the entire Pashtun nation is in grief. Remembering his services and following his path is the duty of every Pashtun. We pray for his forgiveness in the court of God. May Allah Almighty grant patience to his family and all his friends. Amen.

166.right:
Pashtun Atmanzi
21 June 1939
A Pashtun's message of grief on the death of Khan Sahib God Dad Khan
It is a pity that a great leader like Khan Sahib God Dad Khan, who understood the pain and sorrow of the nation's heart and a mujahid for the freedom of the country, who always raised his voice against the oppression and tyranny of the British, is not among us today. His death is a great tragedy for the entire nation. In this grief, not only his family and friends, but all those who dreamed of the independence and prosperity of the country, are in fear and mourning. God Dad Khan Sahib's life and struggle history is a clear example for those who are ready to sacrifice their lives in the path of truth. With his death, a page of the struggle of Pakhtunkhwa was turned. May God have mercy on him and may heaven be his place. Our condolences to his family and the rest of the family.
166.left:
A message of sympathy for the Pashtun
The struggle of Khan Sahib Khudai Dad Khan for the service of the nation is an example that will always be a torch for future generations. He spent a large part of his life for the unity of the Pashtuns and for winning their rights. In his death, not only his family but the entire Pashtun nation is in grief. Remembering his services and following his path is the duty of every Pashtun. We pray for his forgiveness in the court of God. May Allah Almighty grant his family and all his friends the patience to bear this loss. Amen.

Introduction to the subject

Acquaintance for the sake of a goal is all for a purpose; there is no acquaintance without a purpose. If the goal is not achieved, the acquaintance does not remain; if the goal is achieved, the acquaintance does not last until morning. God's works are strange; there was a time when the British and the Russians were bitter enemies. Each side spoke ill of the other's work; in their opinion, the Russians were infidels. And regarding the British infidels, they had spread the notion that there were no greater oppressors in the world than them. As time passed, the British were not recognized as their opponents. The wise and knowledgeable would remain silent for a purpose, but such is their situation. It is known that the Bolsheviks were presenting a very bad map of the world. In India, the British power was sufficient for them, as they considered all of India to be under their influence. And as much as they had among them, there it was recognized among the Pashtuns. Whatever happened in their own country, they committed great oppression with Russia. Whoever understands them knows they were not of the province; in the words of Europe, only the British and Russians had taken the lead. Their efforts were together.

Among the Pashtuns on the border, they started their propaganda. May God make their believers easy. May God make them go away. They would approach the Red government with trickery. The sincerity of Abdul Ghaffar Khan is evident in his commitment to the principles of Islam, seeking paradise rather than the torment of hell.  Ignorance is a barrier to understanding, and the pursuit of knowledge is essential.  The practice of unjustly killing believers is abhorrent, and the protection of the faithful from oppression is paramount.  Love for one’s fellow Muslim is a virtue, and rivalry in the arena should be avoided.  Let us cast off the yoke of slavery and embrace the freedom granted by the Prophet.  May the heart harbor the hope of the Holy Prophet’s intercession.  Let the Qur’an be our guide, and may we follow the example of the Commander of the Faithful, Abdul Ghaffar Khan.
(Al-Abd Ghulam Haider Sahib – Head of the Leaders)

---
India and the Frontier Regions

Efforts to improve the administration of the Indian frontier regions have yielded limited results, as there is little prospect of achieving true independence for the people of the region.  To combat the persistence of slavery and the practice of forced marriage, it is essential to address the underlying causes of these issues.  Therefore, it is necessary to provide young men with opportunities for education and employment, to discourage the continuation of slavery and forced marriage, and to advance the cause of freedom through these means.  Let us strive towards this goal, providing the young generation with the opportunity to inherit the legacy of their ancestors, to claim their own destiny, and to build a future free from oppression.

194.right:
January 1929

This Jirga, which included Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan, Abdul Akbar Khan, Rustam Khan, and other Pashtuns, began in the month of November. In this meeting, it was decided to form the Pashtun "Zalmo Jirga" (Youth Jirga), similar to the "All India Youth League" group in India. The primary purpose of forming this Jirga was to show Pashtuns the path of national unity, independence, and the struggle for their rights. In this meeting, Pashtun leaders emphasized in their speeches that Pashtuns must adopt the path of unity and strive for a common goal. The leaders of this Jirga told the participants that without unity, Pashtuns cannot achieve their rights and must support each other in all circumstances for this goal. Likewise, decisions were made in this meeting to strengthen the cultural and social affairs of the Pashtuns. It is worth noting that the message of unity was spread in the meetings of this Jirga, and it guided Pashtuns to rise as a united force. In the course of this Jirga's work, the need to increase efforts for unity and consensus among the youth in various regions of Pashtunkhwa was mentioned. ---
Pashtun 29

This - that a Pashtun does not recognize it, its profit and loss seem good to him. And if someone understands this fully, then they should look at it with a little more care and thought. For this, it is necessary that there should be much more understanding among the people of the Frontier Province regarding this cliché. Compared to the group in India called the (All India Youth League), there are very distinct differences in the culture and politics of our Pashtun Zalmo Jirga. We know that this is a very necessary task for the Pashtuns, that the Jirga which is for a Pashtun province should have discussions and consultations once again with the people of Pashtunkhwa regarding the creation of such a framework for Pashtun politics and its demands. At this time, when Pashtuns take this responsibility upon themselves for their unity, it is clearly a sign of great pride. For the formation of this group, there is a need for a new and strong unity for the Pashtuns at this time. There is a broad participation of the people in the formation of this Jirga, and the contribution of every single person of the nation in this is acceptable as a national duty. In the formation of this Jirga, it acts as a foundation upon which a step is being taken towards a new phase for the future of the Pashtuns. In September 1929, it was decided to lay the foundation of such a Jirga for the people of the Frontier Province, and in October, it was decided to hold a meeting to form a central Jirga, and a decision on such a form for this Jirga in the province is necessary.

197.right:
On January 25th, there was a meeting in the main mosque in Tank, where people were present, and the respected Khans of the area were also included. In the meeting, we conveyed the message of brotherhood to everyone; with sincerity, we found friendship and affection. On January 12th, people were gathered in a meeting in Leech, and sincere workers from Kohat also took the trouble to come; upon their request for prayer, I reached Peshawar.

On December 12th, I set out again, and to the areas of Badarga, Eiro, the Rani Zani of the area, Kot, Miana, Kot, and Spen Khwar, I went to the valleys and Alindai Dara. There is no doubt that the people are ready, and due to some workers, there is preparation; in Badarga, one gives [support], when these special zones are present. In Miana, due to the workers, a special awakening is emerging; it is possible that in Miana, where Abdul Latif Sahib Afandi and his like-minded people are, an independent Islamic school is also running. I took an examination of the students of these members and these Maliks; the boys were very capable.
197.left:
January 1923

33

And set out on the path; the tour on white donkeys ended, but the tour was very difficult, as the journey was always on mountain passes, and then through very harsh Pashtun deserts; besides this, we were not spared from the rain and the donkey's stubbornness.

Although the middle region is mountainous, the mountains are still inhabited, and the land provides all the necessary supplies for a suitable livelihood; there is also plenty of game here—partridges, chukars, and other things are abundant, and at one time the people of this region lived entirely off this. For a long time, the government's attention has been turned toward these people in a crooked way—there is no doubt about it. If they want to hunt, they are fined five rupees per head for many people, and if they want to hunt small game, there is no prohibition; but if they want to hunt in that place, there is no permission for anything unfair, so that they are deprived of the rights of the land, while outsiders hunt freely. May God show favor to these poor Pashtuns, and may God guide their tribal elders not to sell the freedom, peace, prosperity, and honor of their land to others, not to be swayed by the influence of others, and to form their own jirga for the sake of their own reconciliation.

199.right:
January 1920

I say amen to this statement of my Pashtun brothers of the American nation, who give much money in the path of noble service and love!

The truth is that today, work cannot be done without money. You see, today, any nation that does not have a strong treasury (Bait-ul-Mal) does not have new propaganda, hospitals, and schools for its nation, and cannot serve the country and religion of its people; and if a nation does not have a treasury, then you see that it is equal to nothing else, which is the state of the nation.

If a person asks why Pashtuns do not do as other nations do, this statement is wrong; Pashtuns give much more charity than their means. But their method of charity is such that it is not of immediate benefit to the nation, it is not eternal; undoubtedly, their method of charity is in the form of the past era, but today its benefit is a loss to the nation.

The era has changed; it was necessary for Muslims to have changed too. But they did not change with the era, because instead of benefit, it is a loss for them. In the past, people would serve the nation's religion for free, and you would offer money for the people, you would give it to them; in this way, the service of the nation continued, and the servants also made a living.

But you see today that everything is for money. The era has changed; propaganda, publication, school, imamate—in fact, there is no matter that can be done without money, and such a person will not die who girds his loins to serve the nation for the sake of God. If a person girds his loins, if he is wealthy, like our Pashtuns, he will make a living, but if he is poor, then what?

For this reason, today it is necessary for Muslims to move with the pace of the era, for Pashtuns to form a national treasury with unity like other nations for money, goods, and cash ideas, and to help the nation, and to remove the nation's neediness, and to support the servants of Islam. Because I hope that our Pashtun brothers will imitate the Pashtun brothers of America and will walk in their footsteps.

The day that I was informed by our American brothers about the charity group's page regarding the education of the association, because today the most necessary thing for us is to acquire knowledge, the receipt is below:

British Policy
The acceptance of humility and humiliation in the face of the powerful, and arrogance and violence in the face of weak nations, is clearly explained by past events. During the British occupation of the Rhineland and the Treaty of Sèvres, Italy occupied Abyssinia, and regarding Japan, Germany made an understanding with the Soviet Union contrary to the Anti-Comintern Pact. In the joint Italian and German involvement in the Spanish Civil War, the British government's stance was evident. Following the defeat of the republican government, all the despicable actions of the Franco government are akin to poisonous stings. Japan's blatant humiliation of the British in China is a testament to Britain's decline. The British policy of non-interference is apparent to the whole world, yet Britain has accepted every instance of humiliation and has stood by watching every kind of tragedy.
Now, instead of Japan, in the areas of intervention, Great Britain will show whether it will choose honor or humiliation; this government's humility in accepting this humiliation is apparent to the newspaper columnists of the government. Twelve pieces of advice from Japanese mothers to their daughters at the time of marriage

(1) After marriage, do not consider your mother-in-law as anything other than your own mother. In fact, consider your mother-in-law as your own mother, consider her as your own, and in any situation, do not upset her.
(2) After marriage, when you enter someone's land, consider obedience to them as your duty.
(3) Try not to give your husband a chance to complain.
(4) Do not hold any kind of grudge or enmity with the people of the house. This will ruin your life.
(5) If your husband speaks harshly about something, do not argue back. Instead, try to calm his anger.
(6) Maintain good behavior with your husband's relatives. Do not interfere in every matter or his personal affairs.
(7) Wake up early in the morning and stay awake until late at night. And whatever has happened during the day, do not share it in any gathering.
(8) Manage the household affairs with careful thought. Make it your own habit.
(9) Do not tell the West about the state of Japanese upbringing and the condition of the home.

Advice to the student
(Pashto Master, Mannan School, Bannu)
1- Boy! Pursue your interest in Pashto with the intention of completing your education.
2- Do not let yourself make mistakes, or you will lead a purposeless life tomorrow.
3- Understanding everything is necessary; the decline of life and wealth lies in ignorance. If you do not understand this, you will remain deprived of the world for a long time.
4- Seek knowledge and art; if you act well in your own time, you will be blessed with the fortune of this world and the hereafter.
5- Use knowledge and wisdom in every task, for success lies in this.
6- Read literature in your books, as it is also necessary in education.
7- Have a passion for studying beautiful books so that you may understand knowledge and art.
8- Follow the path of Pashto in your work so that you may maintain your place in the world.
9- Now you know what is needed for Pashto education; serve your country in the light of knowledge.
10- Show your skill in everything and see what is good for your life; take the path of effort for your life.
